citations.verbatim_excerpt: Open https://overpass-api.de/api/interpreter in a new tab ... Paste the code snippet below ... var result = await fetch( "https://overpass-api.de/api/interpreter", { method: "POST",
To support small and well scaling OSM main services, Overpass API is run as a third party service. You can use the public visible instances:

The around filter selects all elements within a certain radius in metres around the elements in the input set. If you provide coordinates, then these coordinates are used instead of the input set. ... (around:radius,latitude,longitude)
::lat Latitude (available for nodes, or in out center mode) | ::lon Longitude (available for nodes, or in out center mode)
The shop=alcohol tag is for a liquor store, a shop selling alcoholic drinks to takeaway. ... USA: Liquor Store, State Liquor Store, Package Store; UK: Off-Licence.
nwr (shorthand for "nodes, ways or relations") followed by one or more filters. The result set is the set of all elements that match the conditions of all the filters.

options.summary: Live query to the public OpenStreetMap Overpass API (https://overpass-api.de/api/interpreter) directly from browser JavaScript, filtering for shop=alcohol (plus shop=wine, shop=beverages) within a radius around the user's coordinates.
pros: Global coverage — works wherever the user actually is, satisfying the telos for any location.
No API key, no registration, no payment — a public, read-only OSM service usable directly.
Callable directly from browser fetch() with no user-operated backend, satisfying assumption A6. Widely used by pure client-side OSM apps (Leaflet overlays, etc.).
Returns name, address tags (addr:* keys), and coordinates; 'out center' mode yields a lat/lon even for way/relation features, enabling distance computation.
shop=alcohol is the canonical OSM tag specifically for liquor/package stores.
cons: Requires network access at use time — this is genuine online behavior, not bundled. Must be captured explicitly so verification expects (and the app degrades gracefully on) network conditions.
Public instance is rate-limited / best-effort; the app must handle slow responses and timeouts.
OSM data completeness varies by region; sparse-data areas may return few or no results, requiring a graceful empty-result message and/or a widening search radius.

options.summary: Bundle a static dataset of liquor-store locations into the app.
pros: Fully offline; no network dependency at use time.
cons: Only covers whatever fixed region was bundled — silently fails (returns wrong or empty results) for any user outside that region. Directly contradicts 'closest to wherever the user actually is'.
Goes stale; stores open and close.
No authoritative, freely-redistributable global liquor-store dataset exists to bundle.

rationale: The telos requires a real, current answer for wherever the user is. A bundled dataset (O2) only works for a pre-chosen region and silently fails elsewhere, which is the failure mode IP1's importance_action_evidence explicitly warns against. The OpenStreetMap Overpass public API (O1) provides global liquor-store coverage via the canonical shop=alcohol tag, requires no API key and no user-operated backend, and is callable directly from browser JavaScript. It does require network access at use time — this is honest, required online behavior and is captured explicitly in section-2's charter and acceptance so verification treats network calls as in-scope rather than a defect. The app must degrade gracefully: on timeout/offline/empty results it shows a clear message and offers the manual-location path and radius widening. This is the production-fidelity-friendly path.
